tasks.title: List Files
tasks.description: Display files and directories in the current directory with detailed information.
tasks.notes: Shows hidden files (starting with .) and displays permissions, owner, size, and modification date.

tasks.title: Copy Files
tasks.description: Copy files or directories from source to destination.
tasks.notes: The -r option is required for copying directories recursively.

tasks.title: Move Files
tasks.description: Move or rename files and directories.
tasks.notes: Can be used for both moving and renaming files.

tasks.title: Delete Files
tasks.description: Remove files or directories.
tasks.notes: WARNING: The -rf options force removal without confirmation. Use with caution!

tasks.title: Create Directories
tasks.description: Create new directories including parent directories if needed.
tasks.notes: The -p option creates parent directories as needed.

tasks.title: Install Software
tasks.description: Install new software packages using DNF.
tasks.notes: AlmaLinux uses DNF package manager, fully compatible with RHEL packages.

tasks.title: Remove Software
tasks.description: Remove installed software packages.
tasks.notes: This removes the package but keeps configuration files.

tasks.title: Update Packages
tasks.description: Update all installed packages to their latest versions.
tasks.notes: DNF handles dependencies automatically and shows what will be updated.

tasks.title: Search Packages
tasks.description: Search for packages by name or description.
tasks.notes: Searches across all enabled repositories for matching packages.

tasks.title: List Available Updates
tasks.description: Check for available package updates without installing.
tasks.notes: Shows list of packages that have updates available in repositories.

tasks.title: Create User
tasks.description: Create a new user account on the system.
tasks.notes: The -m option creates the user's home directory. Then set password with 'sudo passwd username'.

tasks.title: Delete User
tasks.description: Remove a user account from the system.
tasks.notes: The -r option removes the user's home directory and mail spool.

tasks.title: Change Password
tasks.description: Change password for current or specified user.
tasks.notes: Use 'sudo passwd username' to change another user's password.

tasks.title: View Users
tasks.description: Display list of all users on the system.
tasks.notes: Shows system and regular users. For human users, look for /home/ in the path.

tasks.title: View CPU Information
tasks.description: Display detailed information about the CPU.
tasks.notes: Shows architecture, cores, threads, model name, and more.

tasks.title: View Memory Usage
tasks.description: Display current memory (RAM) usage statistics.
tasks.notes: The -h option shows sizes in human-readable format (GB, MB).

tasks.title: View Disk Usage
tasks.description: Display disk space usage for all mounted filesystems.
tasks.notes: Shows used and available space. Use 'du -sh *' to see usage per directory.

tasks.title: View OS Version
tasks.description: Display the AlmaLinux operating system version and details.
tasks.notes: Also try 'hostnamectl' for comprehensive system information including kernel version.

tasks.title: Show IP Address
tasks.description: Display current IP address configuration.
tasks.notes: Look for 'inet' lines to find IPv4 addresses.

tasks.title: Test Connectivity
tasks.description: Test network connectivity to a host.
tasks.notes: The -c option limits the number of ping attempts.

tasks.title: Display Network Interfaces
tasks.description: Show all network interfaces and their status.
tasks.notes: Provides detailed information about network devices and connections.

tasks.title: Check Open Ports
tasks.description: Display list of open ports and listening services.
tasks.notes: Shows TCP (-t) and UDP (-u) listening (-l) ports with numeric (-n) output.

tasks.title: View Running Processes
tasks.description: Display all running processes.
tasks.notes: Shows processes from all users with detailed information.

tasks.title: Kill a Process
tasks.description: Terminate a process by its PID.
tasks.notes: The -9 signal forces immediate termination. Use with caution.

tasks.title: Monitor System Resources
tasks.description: Interactive process viewer and system monitor.
tasks.notes: AlmaLinux includes top by default. Press 'M' to sort by memory usage, 'P' for CPU.

tasks.title: Change File Permissions
tasks.description: Modify read, write, and execute permissions for files and directories.
tasks.notes: 755 gives owner full permissions, group and others read/execute. Use -R for recursive.

tasks.title: Change Ownership
tasks.description: Change the owner and group of files and directories.
tasks.notes: Use -R option for recursive changes in directories.
